title: Duplicate type wells for different horizontal wells
content:
When doing a quick EDA, I noticed there are a few type well files that are exactly the same but assigned to different horizontal wells. In some cases this makes sense (wells that are oriented adjacent or on top of each other), but in other cases the wells are quite distant from each other. Why would wells some distance apart be using exactly the same type well? Is this intentional?

Here are the well pairs with matching type wells, and a picture of how they are oriented compared to other wells with different type wells.

duplicate_groups = [ ['02e7fe5a', '10b89021', '3417285d', '6ae68655', '7993a768', 'bc4381e2', 'ecdab904', 'f021b650', 'f49fdea3', 'f88ddb26'], ['071d7b45', '4463446c'], ['25939962', '8050c789'], ['2f8e53c3', '91db7070'], ['75cd5f11', 'be83e781'], ['7b38844c', 'ed6e6e54'], ['89f1085d', 'aed44918'], ['8b95d6d1', 'a2e8e7f6'], ['a4f989c2', 'd011f41b'], ['add9c322', 'cbe62450'], ['b977be4a', 'c908edd0'], ['cd7f1687', 'fcfcc902'], ['f321a31c', 'fa667be2'], ]

comments.content:
Good catch!

Typically typewell selection is a manual process, the geologist picks the one that is close to the lateral and available at the time. Wells in the project were drilled over 10 years and when you were drilling certain lateral the closest typewell (the one the is close now) could not be available.

Additionally the geologist may take TVT GR data from the nearby interpreted lateral to interpret the next lateral. Sometimes this helps a lot and this approach is called "pseudo-typewell". Some of the typewells in the project are actually "pseudo-typewells" - interpretations from nearby laterals drilled prior to the current one.
Very good catch thanks ! I found 50 unique typewells, please correct me if you find less or more. I add this big picture that illustrate how grouped are the HW by typewells in the X, Y map.
